title: Cota mazandaranica
intro: Cota mazandaranica, also known as Mazandaran's Cota or Mazandaran's yellow chamomile, is a flowering plant in the daisy family (Asteraceae) native to Iran. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antispasmodic properties, which may help alleviate symptoms of insomnia, anxiety, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Cota mazandaranica include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and essential oils, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Herbal preparations made from this plant include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to promote relaxation, calm the nervous system, and soothe digestive issues.
taxonomy: Cota mazandaranica is classified as a species within the plant family Asteraceae. It belongs to the genus Cota, which is a group of small-flowered plants. The genus Cota is further divided into several species, with Cota mazandaranica being one of them. This classification suggests a close relationship with other plants in the same family and genus.
